data.narrative:
Hadean is a Series B-stage frontier-tech company in Defense & Security, having raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—all operating in the same sector at similar or later stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $76M. Peer-median analysis examined six same-sector, same-stage comparables: Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, Defense Unicorns, and one additional peer. The median valuation across this set is $1.2B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B. The stage-progression method likely understates value, as it ignores traction and market positioning captured in peer multiples; the peer-median approach, conversely, anchors to observed market prices for materially similar companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $65M to $1.4B, with a median implied value of $638M. This wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Hadean's commercial traction and defensibility align with established-stage peers valued near $1.2B, or whether earlier-stage risk warrants a material discount. Key uncertainties include revenue run rate, customer concentration, and technology maturity relative to the peer set.
